description: 'noe' marks the rear side of a landmark, whether the object has a back of its own or the scene lends it one. A cat behind a pot may still be plainly visible. Concealment is a separate fact.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper front teeth for voiced dental 'n' and keep the airflow nasal as the lips round around mid-back 'o'. Preserve the hiatus while the lips unround and the tongue moves forward into middle-height 'e'. Stress 'no'.
sound_symbolism: The vowels of 'noe' answer 'neo' (FRONT) in reverse order. This small audible mirror keeps the two ends of the orientation pair connected.
usage_notes: Put 'noe' before a landmark with an inherent or contextually understood front and back. The subject's own orientation is irrelevant.

examples.translation: The cat sleeps behind the pot.
